candidates.why: This is the primary top-line revenue figure for the company, labeled explicitly as Total Revenue.
This is a liability representing revenue received but not yet earned, not the top-line revenue figure.

variables.notes: Selected the explicit income statement Total Revenue as top-line sales.

candidates.why: This represents costs of inventory items used in production and is the best proxy for cost of goods sold among the options.
This is an adjustment item rather than the main COGS line; typically used in combination with raw materials but is not itself the primary COGS row.

candidates.row_label: Raw materials and finished goods - Balancing value
candidates.why: This is clearly marked as a balancing value, not the main expense component, and should not be used as COGS.

variables.notes: Chose the main raw materials and finished goods line as COGS and excluded the balancing and inventory change rows.

candidates.why: Represents personnel-related operating expenses, which are classic SG&A overhead components.
A broad operating overhead bucket, appropriate as part of SG&A components (excluding COGS/D&A).
Another labeled operating expense line, likely part of overhead/SG&A rather than COGS.
Share-based compensation is typically included within SG&A and should be captured as an SG&A component.

candidates.row_label: Brands, Patents, Trademarks, Marketing & Artistic Intangibles - Gross
candidates.why: This is a balance sheet asset account, not a period SG&A expense; should not be classified as SG&A.
Represents accrued wage expenses, which relate to personnel/operating expenses and are SG&A in nature although shown on the balance sheet.

variables.notes: No single explicit SG&A line; selected multiple overhead-related components and excluded the intangible asset line.

candidates.why: Explicitly labeled research & development expense, even if supplemental; this is the correct R&D line.

variables.notes: Clear, explicit R&D expense line selected.

candidates.row_label: Gain on remeasurement of previously held equity interest in business combination
candidates.why: This is a remeasurement gain on an equity interest, not an interest expense item.
This is the primary interest expense line and best represents XINT.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities
candidates.why: Interest on lease liabilities is a component of interest expense but more specific; total interest expense is better captured by the broader Interest Expenses line.
Represents profit attributable to non-controlling interests, not interest expense.
Same as non-controlling interests; equity attribution, not interest expense.
A net figure could represent interest expense net of income, but it appears unused (nan) here; the explicit Interest Expenses line is preferred.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental
candidates.why: Supplemental disclosure of lease interest; secondary to the main Interest Expenses line for XINT.
Balance sheet liability, not an interest expense flow item.
Balance sheet liability, not an interest expense flow item.
Balance sheet liability, not an interest expense flow item.
Balance sheet liability, not an interest expense flow item.
Equity component, not interest expense.

variables.notes: Chose the main Interest Expenses line as XINT; lease interest and supplemental items are components but not used separately here.

candidates.row_label: Gain on remeasurement of previously held equity interest in business combination
candidates.why: This is a P&L gain item, not an equity balance.

candidates.row_label: Gain From Fair Value Measurement of Equity Instruments
candidates.why: This is a P&L fair value gain, not a book equity balance.

candidates.row_label: Loss From Fair Value Measurement of Equity Instruments
candidates.why: This is a P&L fair value loss, not a book equity balance.

candidates.row_label: Change in fair value of equity instruments
candidates.why: This is an OCI-related flow, not the stock of equity.
Represents an asset investment in equity instruments, not the company’s own book equity.
Component of equity but not total book equity; better to use total equity attributable to owners or overall equity.
Another component of equity, but not the total book equity figure.

candidates.row_label: Equity attributable to owners of the parent
candidates.why: Represents total book equity attributable to shareholders (owners of the parent), matching the BE definition well.
Represents total equity including non-controlling interests; close but slightly less precise for shareholders’ book equity than equity attributable to owners.

final_choice.row_label: Equity attributable to owners of the parent

variables.notes: Selected Equity attributable to owners of the parent as the book equity measure, excluding non-controlling interests, which are handled separately via MIB.

candidates.why: This is the income statement portion of profit attributable to non-controlling interests; useful but the balance sheet figure is preferred for MIB (equity).
Same as the above, profit attributable to NCI, not the equity balance itself.
This is the balance sheet line for minority/non-controlling interests, which is exactly the MIB definition.

variables.notes: Chose the balance sheet Non-controlling interests line as the minority interest (MIB) measure.
